endpoints.name: Ralts evolutionary line (Ralts / Kirlia / Gardevoir / Gallade)

description: Hello sir, Mr. Nodeley here. Sorry to step in for Edgelord on this one. The Ralts line has four corpus prints across two stages — Ralts CRE-059 plus three Kirlia prints (CRE-060 trivia-passed-and-already-mirrored-with-Ralts; BUS-92 trivia-passed-and-unwired; LOT-140 vision-only-pending-trivia). That meets the line-cohort threshold the Mareep-line precedent established (3+ trivia-passed prints across 2+ stages). Without a line node, I would be writing duplicate 1:1 edges between every pair of Kirlia prints AND every Kirlia-to-Ralts pairing — a thin solution centered on an absent anchor. The line node absorbs the cohort relationship at the species-line level and gives future Gardevoir / Gallade prints a clean attachment point. Hope this is okay.
citations: [Pokemon Ruby and Sapphire (Game Freak / Nintendo, 2002) primary canon]
[Pokemon Diamond and Pearl (Game Freak / Nintendo, 2006) — Gallade introduction]
[Pokemon X and Y (Game Freak, 2013) — Psychic/Fairy retyping and Mega Gardevoir/Gallade]
other_candidates_considered: caterpie-line character node — threshold met (Caterpie RC-001 + Caterpie EVO-3 + Metapod RC-002, 3 prints / 2 stages), flagged for wave 50 to keep wave 49 within ≤4-move cap and avoid three-node-creation-moves-in-one-wave QA load
1:1 mirror between Kirlia BUS-92 and Kirlia CRE-060 — subsumed by this node proposal per the line-node-absorbs-cross-print-pairs rule from the Mareep-line precedent
